content:
Signal Forms went stable in Angular 22. That doesn’t mean your team should switch it means you finally have to decide. Here’s the framework nobody else has written.

8 min read
16 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Signal Forms are finally stable in Angular 22, but should you actually replace Reactive Forms?
Most articles explain how Signal Forms work. Very few tell you whether switching is worth it.
After comparing the official Angular documentation, migration guides, and real-world implementations, here’s the framework I’d use to decide.
I’ve been building Angular applications professionally for over three years and have worked with Reactive Forms in production across multiple projects. When Signal Forms hit developer preview, I learned the API from the official docs and wrote about it.
Now Angular 22 (June 3, 2026) has made them stable, and every Angular team I know is asking the same question:
Do we switch?
Plenty of articles explain Signal Forms. The official docs even compare the APIs side by side. But explaining isn’t deciding and the two most thoughtful comparisons out there were written while Signal…
